metadata.name: Janaka
metadata.primary_role: King of Mithila (Videha) / Father of Sita / Philosopher-king and Rajarishi
metadata.ethnicity_cultural_context: Ancient Indian (Videha kingdom, Mithila — present-day Bihar/Nepal border)
metadata.divine_origin: A Rajarishi (sage-king) who attained Brahma-jnana (supreme spiritual knowledge) while ruling — famous for his equanimity

timelines.context_description: The wise king of Mithila who found baby Sita while ploughing a sacred field, and organized the Swayamvara with Shiva's bow.

physical_appearance.skin_tone: Warm medium-brown complexion, healthy and radiant with the glow of a man who has mastered both worldly duties and spiritual wisdom
physical_appearance.height: Medium height, upright and dignified, the composed bearing of a philosopher-king
physical_appearance.build: Neither lean nor heavy — a balanced build of a man who meditates and also governs, strong hands from personally ploughing the sacred field, settled comfortable strength
physical_appearance.facial_features: Serene intelligent face with large calm contemplative eyes, broad forehead of a thinker, neatly trimmed grey-flecked beard, gentle warm smile that reflects inner peace, crow's feet from years of wise smiling, the face of a man who found a daughter in a furrow and never questioned the miracle
physical_appearance.hair: Silver-grey hair neatly groomed, simple royal crown resting lightly on his head
physical_appearance.distinguishing_marks: His hands show the unique combination of a farmer's calluses and a scribe's ink-stains — he ploughs and philosophizes with equal dedication

wardrobe_and_adornments.clothing: Simple but regal white and gold robes — less ostentatious than Ayodhya's royalty, reflecting Mithila's more intellectual culture, a philosopher's simplicity with a king's dignity
wardrobe_and_adornments.jewelry: A modest golden crown of Mithila (simpler than Ayodhya's grand mukut), a sacred thread, pearl necklace, simple gold earrings, a philosophical restraint in adornment
wardrobe_and_adornments.weapons_carried: A ceremonial scepter — symbol of office rather than a weapon, though he was trained in warfare

audio_and_voice_characteristics.tone: Calm, contemplative, warm, speaks with the measured ease of a man who has resolved all inner conflicts — his voice is the voice of a teacher-king
audio_and_voice_characteristics.pitch: Warm steady baritone — measured and unhurried, with an almost meditative quality, gentle and accessible
audio_and_voice_characteristics.speech_style: Speaks in philosophical observations, every statement carries multiple layers of wisdom, addresses Rama with the delight of recognizing divinity, speaks of Sita with a father's tender love, uses simple words for profound truths
audio_and_voice_characteristics.emotional_baseline: Serene equanimity, paternal love (especially for Sita), philosophical detachment that paradoxically allows deeper engagement with life

cinematic_generation_prompts.visual_prompt: Warm cinematic portrait of Philosopher-King Janaka of Mithila, age 50. Medium-brown complexion with a radiant healthy glow. Serene intelligent face with calm contemplative eyes, grey-flecked beard, warm wise smile. Silver-grey hair under a modest golden crown. Wearing simple white and gold royal robes. Seated on a modest but elegant throne in the philosophical court of Mithila, with scholars and students around. Warm natural daylight, open-air court with simple elegance. 3D Pixar style, warm contemplative lighting, photorealistic, 8k.
cinematic_generation_prompts.audio_prompt: A warm, steady male baritone voice with meditative quality. Measured and unhurried, philosophical depth in simple words. Age approximately 50 years.
